title: cephalotaxaceae
intro: The Cephalotaxaceae rank is a family of plants, specifically a group of coniferous evergreen trees and shrubs. This family is characterized by its unique combination of needle-like leaves and distinctive seed cones. The Cephalotaxaceae family is relatively small, containing around 10-15 species, and is primarily found in the mountainous regions of East Asia. Some notable species within this family include the Japanese plum yew (Cephalotaxus harringtonia) and the Chinese plum yew (Cephalotaxus fortunei).
